old_string: 'expected': '<WHAT you expect by next cycle — focus on SYSTEM-OBSERVABLE outcomes (task completions, queue state, system health). Include reactive dispatch probability: if Mev has been active recently, predict "likely reactive dispatch" instead of "idle". For Mev-dependent outcomes, use conditional framing: "IF Mev responds, THEN X". Do NOT predict WHEN Mev will respond — only predict what the system will do in response.>',
new_string: 'expected': '<Write TWO explicit branches separated by " | IF_MEV: ". DEFAULT: system-observable state prediction assuming no external input (queue counts, task completions, health changes). IF_MEV: what Otto will do if Mev sends a message or unblocks something. Score the DEFAULT branch if idle, score the IF_MEV branch if Mev responded — a prediction that matches EITHER branch is "matched", not "partial". Do NOT include probability percentages for Mev responses — they are untestable on single events and inflate partials. Focus on observable outcomes only.>',
reason: RL2F active-only stuck at 34% for 85 cycles. Root cause: 56% partials from Mev-behavior probability predictions that are untestable on single events. This restructures EXPECTED to two scoreable branches (DEFAULT for idle, IF_MEV for response), eliminating probability percentages that guarantee partial scoring.
